mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold signals a strengthening demand for residential real estate, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain, which reduces the availability of goods and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening-supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in payroll employment signals a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in strengthening-labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate signals a monetary easing cycle, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production and a 1-month decline in housing units sold signals a weakening supply chain and a decline in residential real estate demand, which reduces the availability of goods and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in weakening-supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening-labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the consumer price index signals a monetary tightening cycle, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
